rulings.Case Title: DANISHIA DAVID VS. CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: / Notice Of Motion And Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Danishia Davis
rulings.Rulings: "Matter on the Discovery calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2024, Line 1 DEFENDANTs MALCOLM YEUNG and CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER,INC's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff Danishia Davis. Off calendar. The moving party and opposing party failed to provide courtesy copies in compliance with SF Local Rule 2.7B. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN BRUNO VS. COMMUNITY FORWARD SF, INC., A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Entire Action Pursuant To An Individual Settlement And Crc 3.770
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2024, Line 9, PLAINTIFF KAREN BRUNO , AN INDIVIDUAL, ON BEHALF OF HERSELF AND ON BEHALF OF ALL PERSONS SIMILARLY SITUATED's Motion To Dismiss Entire Action Pursuant To An Individual Settlement And CRC 3.770. Plaintiff Karen Bruno's Motion To Dismiss Entire Action Pursuant To An Individual Settlement And CRC 3.770 to which no opposition was received is GRANTED as requested. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL POWELL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 19, 2024, Line 10, 2- PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT. Plaintiff Daniel Powell's motion to strike demurrer is DENIED. Under CCP Section 436, the Court may "strike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter inserted in any pleading," or "strike out all or any part of any pleading not drawn in conformity with the laws of this state, a court rule, or an order of the court." (CCP Section 436(a)(b).) CCP Section 435 states, "A notice of motion to strike a demurrer, or a portion thereof, shall set the hearing thereon concurrently with the hearing on the demurrer." Plaintiff moves to "STRIKE this FRIVOLOUS demurrer from Delaying or Blocking Plaintiff's Righteous Action Herein, for any longer than procedurally necessary, hereafter." Plaintiff has not met his standard on the motion to strike to identify what irrelevant, false, or improper matter he seeks to have stricken. If the court assumes Plaintiff is moving to strike the entire demurrer, he does not provide any explanation, authority, or reasoning as to why the court should do that. Simply asserting the demurrer is frivolous does not meet the standard for a motion to strike. In any event, the court has found the demurrer meritorious, meaning Plaintiff's contention that it is frivolous has been rejected. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)
